mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening residential investment and consumer wealth effects, which propagate into lower forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity markets price slower demand growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value fell from 1032.0 to revised 1013.0 near decision date
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or negative HOUST trend periods show positive mean forward returns with t-test p<0.05 against the null of no difference.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration consistent with an emerging recession, causing risk-averse repricing of equities and negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage_value 5.5 near decision date, up from prior readings
failure_condition: When trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10) across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is falling while CPI (CPIAUCSL) is simultaneously rising, monetary easing loses its stimulative power because inflation constrains further rate cuts, producing a stagflationary environment that generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% with CPIAUCSL vintage 215.132 rising near decision date
regime_hint: stagflationary monetary easing constrained by inflation
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS-down/CPI-up regime shows non-negative mean 60d forward returns, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is not significant beyond either single-series proxy (delta IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, a deceleration in industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals an approaching supply-side slowdown and margin compression for firms, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 111.18 versus revised 100.88, indicating measurement instability near cycle peak
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle windows, INDPRO deceleration fails to precede negative 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over 2-year rolling windows within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects continued labor demand and consumer income support, which sustains near-term risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value 137754 versus revised 137851, indicating small upward payroll revision near decision date
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months show forward 5d SPX returns not significantly different from negative PAYEMS growth months (t-test p>0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below a critical threshold of 1000 thousand units, it signals a structural collapse in residential construction demand that historically precedes broader economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1013.0 approaching the 1000-unit threshold as of April 2008 vintage
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following the HOUST sub-1000 trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears when tested out-of-sample post-2008.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in retail sales growth (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) jointly indicate a consumer demand collapse that individual series alone do not fully capture, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as aggregate consumption weakens.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 0 AND UNRATE rising
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value 385448 vs revised 368255 showing downward revision, alongside UNRATE at 5.5%
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-down/UNRATE-up condition fails to produce more negative mean 60d returns than either single-series signal alone (interaction Rank IC gain < 0.02), or triggered instances show non-negative mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is already near cyclical lows and CPI (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated, markets interpret further easing as ineffective against stagflation risk, generating short-horizon negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days around monetary policy signals.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.98% with CPIAUCSL revised_value 215.208 near decision date, indicating limited policy room amid persistent inflation
failure_condition: Within the low-rate, high-inflation regime, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p>0.10) relative to baseline, or the effect reverses sign when tested across independent stagflationary sub-periods.
